name: poly_bevel_G1_halfshared_tent
description: Polygon Bevel (mesh.bevel, edit-mode Polygons) GROUP shared-corner accumulator, half-shared branch (internalCnt==1) -- task 0458 Phase 1. Two adjacent quads sharing one ridge edge A-B, built as a NON-90-degree-dihedral, unequal-adjacent-edge-length asymmetric 'tent' (raw mesh, not a cube) so the group shift accumulator's AVE_N law (k*N/|N|^2 over the incident selected-face CORNER unit normals) is exercised off the axis-aligned degeneracy every prior grouped-poly-bevel fixture happened to sit on (a cube corner's incident normals are mutually orthogonal, where AVE_N collapses to the naive Sigma(unit normal) sum -- passing there proves nothing about the general law). group=true, inset=0.1, shift=0.1. Reference topology: 6v/2f -> 12v/8f -- the shared ridge collapses to ONE new vertex per endpoint (not one per face), its OTHER 4 standalone corners keep vibe3d's existing independent per-face inset+shift, and the ridge's own internal edge is suppressed (no bridge quad across it, only the 2 faces' remaining 3 boundary edges each get a bridge).
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's polygon-bevel op (task 0458 Phase 0 dump-oracle poly_bevel_G1_halfshared_tent) -- a deliberately asymmetric raw_mesh so the shared-corner accumulator's amplified-average-normal law (recovered, toolcards/poly.bevel/findings.md) is pinned off the cube-corner degeneracy poly_bevel_corner.json sits on. Predicted-vs-measured to 6.2e-9 (float32 floor).

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor polygon-bevel tool, headless scripted op-dump)
provenance.notes: Phase-0 dump-oracle for the group shift accumulator's internalCnt==1 (half-shared) branch. bevelFacesByMask's pre-0458 naive Sigma(unit faceNormal) shift term coincides with the reference's AVE_N=k*N/|N|^2 ONLY when the incident corner normals are mutually orthogonal (every cube-corner fixture before this task) -- this raw_mesh is asymmetric specifically to discriminate the two laws.
